text: This study focuses on the Circle of Concerned African Women Theologians contribution towards changing the face of higher theological education in Africa. Using the framework of the African Women’s Theology the study examined the role of the Circle in the promotion of gender justice, equity and solidarity of gender marginalized in the male dominated theological academic institutions. It looks at the Circle achievements by highlighting the struggle and achievement for the inclusion of gender sensitivity in the theological curriculum and the mentoring of young women scholars and futre African scholars. This qualitative study adopted the Circle of Concerned African Women Theologians for Methodist Women approach using the African Women’s Theology as a hermeneutical tool to critique gender representation and structural inequalities in religious higher education.
